classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: John of Damascus: Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
Concerning light, fire, the luminaries, sun, moon and stars.

metadata.author: John of Damascus: Exposition of the Orthodox Faith

ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: Concerning light, fire, the luminaries, sun, moon and stars.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Fire is one of the four elements, light and with a greater tendency to ascend than the others.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It has the power of burning and also of giving light, and it was made by the Creator on the first day.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the divine Scripture says, And God said, Let there be light, and there was light.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Fire is not a different thing from what light is, as some maintain.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Others again hold that this fire of the universe is above the air and call it ether.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: In the beginning, then, that is to say on the first day, God created light, the ornament and glory of the whole visible creation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For take away light and all things remain in undistinguishable darkness, incapable of displaying their native beauty.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And God called the light day, but the darkness He called night.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Further, darkness is not any essence, but an accident: for it is simply absence of light.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The air, indeed, has not light in its essence.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It was, then, this very absence of light from the air that God called darkness: and it is not the essence of air that is darkness, but the absence of light which clearly is rather an accident than an essence.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And, indeed, it was not night, but day, that was first named, so that day is first and after that comes night.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Night, therefore, follows day.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And from the beginning of day till the next day is one complete period of day and night.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the Scripture says, And the evening and the morning were one day.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: When, therefore, in the first three days the light was poured forth and reduced at the divine command, both day and night came to pass.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But on the fourth day God created the great luminary, that is, the sun, to have rule and authority over the day: for it is by it that day is made: for it is day when the sun is above the earth, and the duration of a day is the course of the sun over the earth from its rising till its setting.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And He also created the lesser luminaries, that is, the moon and the stars, to have rule and authority over the night, and to give light by night.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For it is night when the sun is under the earth, and the duration of night is the course of the sun under the earth from its rising till its setting.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The moon, then, and the stars were set to lighten the night: not that they are in the daytime under the earth, for even by day stars are in the heaven over the earth but the sun conceals both the stars and the moon by the greater brilliance of its light and prevents them from being seen.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: On these luminaries the Creator bestowed the first-created light: not because He was in need of other light, but that that light might not remain idle.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For a luminary is not merely light, but a vessel for containing light.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: There are, we are told, seven planets amongst these luminaries, and these move in a direction opposite to that of the heaven: hence the name planets.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For, while they say that the heaven moves from east to west, the planets move from west to east; but the heaven bears the seven planets along with it by its swifter motion.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Now these are the names of the seven planets: Luna, Mercury, Venus, Sol, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, and in each zone of heaven is, we are told, one of these seven planets:

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: In the first and highest Saturn

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: In the second Jupiter

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: In the sixth Mercury

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: In the seventh and lowest Luna.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The course which the Creator appointed for them to run is unceasing and remaineth fixed as He established them.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the divine David says, The moon and the stars which Thou establishedst, and by the word ‘establishedst,’ he referred to the fixity and unchangeableness of the order and series granted to them by God.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For He appointed them for seasons, and signs, and days and years.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It is through the Sun that the four seasons are brought about.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And the first of these is spring: for in it God created all things, and even down to the present time its presence is evidenced by the bursting of the flowers into bud, and this is the equinoctial period, since day and night each consist of twelve hours.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It is caused by the sun rising in the middle, and is mild and increases the blood, and is warm and moist, and holds a position midway between winter and summer, being warmer and drier than winter, but colder and moister than summer.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: This season lasts from March 21st till June 24th.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Next, when the rising of the sun moves towards more northerly parts, the season of summer succeeds, which has a place midway between spring and autumn, combining the warmth of spring with the dryness of autumn: for it is dry and warm, and increases the yellow bile.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: In it falls the longest day, which has fifteen hours, and the shortest night of all, having only nine hours.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: This season lasts from June 24th till September 25th.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Then when the sun again returns to the middle, autumn takes the place of summer.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It has a medium amount of cold and heat, dryness and moisture, and holds a place midway between summer and winter, combining the dryness of summer with the cold of winter.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For it is cold and dry, and increases the black bile.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: This season, again, is equinoctial, both day and night consisting of twelve hours, and it lasts from September 25th till December 25th.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And when the rising of the sun sinks to its smallest and lowest point, i.e. the south, winter is reached, with its cold and moisture.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It occupies a place midway between autumn and spring, combining the cold of autumn and the moisture of spring.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: In it falls the shortest day, which has only nine hours, and the longest night, which has fifteen: and it lasts from December 25th till March 21st.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the Creator made this wise provision that we should not pass from the extreme of cold, or heat, or dryness, or moisture, to the opposite extreme, and thus incur grievous maladies.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For reason itself teaches us the danger of sudden changes.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: So, then, it is the sun that makes the seasons, and through them the year: it likewise makes the days and nights, the days when it rises and is above the earth, and the nights when it sets below the earth: and it bestows on the other luminaries, both moon and stars, their power of giving forth light.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Further, they say that there are in the heaven twelve signs made by the stars, and that these move in an opposite direction to the sun and moon, and the other five planets, and that the seven planets pass across these twelve signs.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Further, the sun makes a complete month in each sign and traverses the twelve signs in the same number of months.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: These, then, are the names of the twelve signs and their respective months:—

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Ram, which receives the sun on the 21st of March.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Bull, on the 23rd of April.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Twins, on the 24th of May.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Crab, on the 24th of June.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Virgin, on the 25th of July.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Scales, on the 25th of September.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Scorpion, on the 25th of October.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Archer, on the 25th of November.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Capricorn, on the 25th of December.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Aquarius, on the 25th of January.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Fish, on the 24th of February.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But the moon traverses the twelve signs each month, since it occupies a lower position and travels through the signs at a quicker rate.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For if you draw one circle within another, the inner one will be found to be the lesser: and so it is that owing to the moon occupying a lower position its course is shorter and is sooner completed.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Now the Greeks declare that all our affairs are controlled by the rising and setting and collision of these stars, viz., the sun and moon: for it is with these matters that astrology has to do.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But we hold that we get from them signs of rain and drought, cold and heat, moisture and dryness, and of the various winds, and so forth, but no sign whatever as to our actions.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For we have been created with free wills by our Creator and are masters over our own actions.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Indeed, if all our actions depend on the courses of the stars, all we do is done of necessity: and necessity precludes either virtue or vice.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But if we possess neither virtue nor vice, we do not deserve praise or punishment, and God, too, will turn out to be unjust, since He gives good things to some and afflicts others.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Nay, He will no longer continue to guide or provide for His own creatures, if all things are carried and swept along in the grip of necessity.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And the faculty of reason will be superfluous to us: for if we are not masters of any of our actions, deliberation is quite superfluous.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Reason, indeed, is granted to us solely that we might take counsel, and hence all reason implies freedom of will.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And, therefore, we hold that the stars are not the causes of the things that occur, nor of the origin of things that come to pass, nor of the destruction of those things that perish.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: They are rather signs of showers and changes of air.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But, perhaps, some one may say that though they are not the causes of wars, yet they are signs of them.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And, in truth, the quality of the air which is produced by sun, and moon, and stars, produces in various ways different temperaments, and habits, and dispositions.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But the habits are amongst the things that we have in our own hands, for it is reason that rules, and directs, and changes them.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It often happens, also, that comets arise.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: These are signs of the death of kings, and they are not any of the stars that were made in the beginning, but are formed at the same time by divine command and again dissolved.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And so not even that star which the Magi saw at the birth of the Friend and Saviour of man, our Lord, Who became flesh for our sake, is of the number of those that were made in the beginning.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And this is evidently the case because sometimes its course was from east to west, and sometimes from north to south; at one moment it was hidden, and at the next it was revealed: which is quite out of harmony with the order and nature of the stars.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It must be understood, then, that the moon derives its light from the sun; not that God was unable to grant it light of its own, but in order that rhythm and order may be unimpressed upon nature, one part ruling, the other being ruled, and that we might thus be taught to live in community and to share our possessions with one another, and to be under subjection, first to our Maker and Creator, our God and Master, and then also to the rulers set in authority over us by Him: and not to question why this man is ruler and not I myself, but to welcome all that comes from God in a gracious and reasonable spirit.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The sun and the moon, moreover, suffer eclipse, and this demonstrates the folly of those who worship the creature in place of the Creator, and teaches us how changeable and alterable all things are.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For all things are changeable save God, and whatever is changeable is liable to corruption in accordance with the laws of its own nature.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Now the cause of the eclipse of the sun is that the body of the moon is interposed like a partition-wall and casts a shadow, and prevents the light from being shed down on us: and the extent of the eclipse is proportional to the size of the moon’s body that is found to conceal the sun.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But do not marvel that the moon’s body is the smaller.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For many declare that the sun is many times larger even than the earth, and the holy Fathers say that it is equal to the earth: yet often a small cloud, or even a small hill or a wall quite conceals it.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The eclipse of the moon, on the other hand, is due to the shadow the earth casts on it when it is a fifteen days’ moon and the sun and moon happen to be at the opposite poles of the highest circle, the sun being under the earth and the moon above the earth.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the earth casts a shadow and the sun’s light is prevented from illuminating the moon, and therefore it is then eclipsed.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It should be understood that the moon was made full by the Creator, that is, a fifteen days’ moon: for it was fitting that it should be made complete.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But on the fourth day, as we said, the sun was created.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Therefore the moon was eleven days in advance of the sun, because from the fourth to the fifteenth day there are eleven days.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Hence it happens that in each year the twelve months of the moon contain eleven days fewer than the twelve months of the sun.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the twelve months of the sun contain three hundred and sixty-five and a quarter days, and so because the quarter becomes a whole, in four years an extra day is completed, which is called bis-sextile.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And that year has three hundred and sixty-six days.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The years of the moon, on the other hand, have three hundred and fifty-four days.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For the moon wanes from the time of its origin, or renewal, till it is fourteen and three-quarter days’ old, and proceeds to wane till the twenty-ninth and a half day, when it is completely void of light.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And then when it is once more connected with the sun it is reproduced and renewed, a memorial of our resurrection.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Thus in each year the moon gives away eleven days to the sun, and so in three years the intercalary month of the Hebrews arises, and that year comes to consist of thirteen months, owing to the addition of these eleven days.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It is evident that both sun and moon and stars are compound and liable to corruption according to the laws of their various natures.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But of their nature we are ignorant.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Some, indeed, say that fire when deprived of matter is invisible, and thus, that when it is quenched it vanishes altogether.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Others, again, say that when it is quenched it is transformed into air.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The circle of the zodiac has an oblique motion and is divided into twelve sections called zodia, or signs: each sign has three divisions of ten each, i.e. thirty divisions, and each division has sixty very minute subdivisions.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The heaven, therefore, has three hundred and sixty-five degrees: the hemisphere above the earth and that below the earth each having one hundred and eighty degrees.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The abodes of the planets.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Ram and the Scorpion are the abode of Mars: the Bull and the Scales, of Venus: the Twins and the Virgin, of Mercury: the Crab, of the Moon: the Lion, of the Sun: the Archer and the Fish, of Jupiter: Capricorn and Aquarius, of Saturn.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The Ram has the altitude of the Sun: the Bull, of the Moon: the Crab, of Jupiter: the Virgin, of Mars: the Scales, of Saturn: Capricorn, of Mercury: the Fish, of Venus.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: The phases of the moon.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It is in conjunction whenever it is in the same degree as the sun: it is born when it is fifteen degrees distant from the sun: it rises when it is crescent-shaped, and this occurs twice, at which times it is sixty degrees distant from the sun: it is half-full twice, when it is ninety degrees from the sun: twice it is gibbous, when it is one hundred and twenty degrees from the sun: it is twice a full moon, giving full light, when it is a hundred and fifty degrees from the sun: it is a complete moon when it is a hundred and eighty degrees distant from the sun.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: We say twice, because these phases occur both when the moon waxes and when it wanes.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: In two and a half days the moon traverses each sign.
